description_long: This is a headless stone sculpture of a seated human figure, likely representing a meditative or religious pose. The figure sits cross-legged with the right hand resting on the knee and the left hand placed in the lap, suggesting a traditional pose often seen in Buddhist or Hindu statuary. The surface is weathered and shows signs of age, with a rough, matte finish and some visible chipping and erosion. The back of the sculpture is broad and rounded, with the shoulders and torso clearly defined, though the arms are partially missing. The overall form is solid and heavy, with no visible internal cavities or openings. The coloration is a pale, mottled beige with subtle variations due to the stone material and aging.
description: A weathered, headless stone sculpture of a seated human figure in a meditative pose, with a rough, pale surface and cross-legged posture.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cross-legged pose, missing head, and right hand resting on the knee.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the arm position and the curvature of the torso and back.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the broad, rounded back and shoulders, with no visible details on the back.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the left hand in the lap and the overall thickness of the torso.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background and base suggest an indoor or controlled environment.
